alzio.detect 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Introduction to alzio.detect

    Compute MCI-to-dementia progression risk score

    compute_progression_risk.Rd

    Estimates the probability of progression from Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI) to Alzheimer's dementia within a given time horizon. Uses a composite risk score derived from logistic regression coefficients consistent with ADNI-based progression models (Li et al., 2019; Gomperts et al., 2013).

    compute_progression_risk(
  age,
  mmse,
  cdr_sb,
  apoe4,
  abeta_status,
  time_horizon = 36
)

    Arguments

    age

    Age in years.

    mmse

    MMSE total score (0–30).

    cdr_sb

    Clinical Dementia Rating sum of boxes.

    apoe4

    Logical, whether at least one APOE ε4 allele is present.

    abeta_status

    CSF or amyloid PET status: "positive" or "negative".

    time_horizon

    Months for the risk projection (default 36). Currently used to calibrate the intercept; standard model is validated at 36 months.

    Value

    A list with elements:

    • risk_score: Estimated probability (0–1) of progression to dementia within time_horizon months.

    • risk_category: "low" (<20\ (20–50\

    Details

    The linear predictor is constructed as: $$LP = \beta_0 + \beta_1 \times \text{age} + \beta_2 \times \text{MMSE} + \beta_3 \times \text{CDR-SB} + \beta_4 \times \text{APOE4} + \beta_5 \times \text{A}\beta\text{+}$$

    Coefficients are calibrated to approximate a ~30% 3-year progression rate in a representative MCI cohort with 50% amyloid positivity.

    References

    Li K, Chan W, Doody RS, et al. (2019) A risk score for predicting progression from mild cognitive impairment to Alzheimer's disease. Journal of Alzheimer's Disease 71(1):S93–S103. doi:10.3233/JAD-181025

    Gomperts SN, Locascio JJ, Hyman BT, et al. (2013) A composite risk score for progression to Alzheimer's disease from mild cognitive impairment. Alzheimer Disease & Associated Disorders 27(3):211–217. doi:10.1097/WAD.0b013e31826a3d21

    Barnes DE, Beiser AS, Lee A, et al. (2014) Development and validation of a brief dementia screening indicator for primary care. Neurology 82(3):212–218. doi:10.1212/WNL.0000000000000037

    Contents
